title: oxydendrum arboreum
intro: Oxydendrum arboreum, also known as the sourwood tree, is a deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States. The tree's flowers and leaves have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Oxydendrum arboreum include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, and its flowers are also used to make a sweet, sour syrup.
